carbon_policy_signal.item: EU ETS review by Commission scheduled July 2026; Poland signals political resistance to ambition level and exemption scope.
carbon_policy_signal.why_it_matters: Review outcome signals ambition trajectory; Poland coalition risk suggests widened exemptions or Member State opt-outs, reducing EUA scarcity premium and flattening power generation marginal cost curve.

risk_flags: GB Power 125.9 EUR/MWh (91st pctile) on 5.82% daily jump — unsustainable if renewables or interconnect capacity normalizes.
EU Storage at 40.09% (17th pctile, 14pp below seasonal) — refill pace critical; if summer demand surprises upward, H2 gas call extends and EUA margin call tightens.
EU ETS review July with Poland-led skepticism — EUA (33.6 EUR/tCO2, +9% MoM) risks 8–12% correction if review signals weakened ambition or exemption widening.
US LNG pipeline capacity +44.9 Bcf/d planned 2026–27 — if ramp completes, TTF arb compression to 2–4 EUR/MWh front-end, pressuring power spreads.

base.summary: GB power normalizes on renewable recovery; storage refill steady; EUA trades ahead of July review.

downside.summary: Renewables surge; GB interconnect capacity returns; EU storage catches seasonal norm. EUA dips 5–7% on Poland coalition weakness.

upside.summary: Cold snap or renewable curtailment extends GB spike; storage refill disappoints. EUA rallies 6–8% pre-review on supply risk.

themes: GB power extreme; EU storage structural deficit
EUA review July amid Polish political headwinds
US LNG export ramp compresses European arb
Tech demand mandate backstops long-term power premiums
top_drivers: GB Power at 91st-percentile (125.9 EUR/MWh, +5.8% daily) on supply tightness; interconnect or renewable outage absorbed into premium.
EU Storage 14pp below seasonal (40.09% full) signals structural H2 thermal call and EUA upside into July review.
US LNG export ramp (44.9 Bcf/d 2026–27) threatens TTF arb compression to 2–4 EUR/MWh, flattening Cal+1 curve.
top_takeaway: GB Power at 91st-percentile on tight supply; EU storage 14pp below seasonal norm signals H2 thermal call and EUA upside despite July review risk.

watchlist.why: EUA curve volatility trigger; Poland coalition risk signals Member State fragmentation on carbon ambition.
14pp seasonal deficit constrains summer inventory buffer; poor refill locks in elevated H2 thermal dispatch and EUA structural premium.
91st-percentile spike unsustainable without demand destruction or renewable curtailment relief; mean-reversion risk 15–20%.

watchlist.metric: US LNG export ramp (2026–27 terminal approval + capacity)
watchlist.why: 44.9 Bcf/d additions flatten European LNG arb; monitors TTF downside and Cal+1 curve shape flattening.

watchlist_dated.name: EU ETS Review (Commission)
watchlist_dated.why: EUA front-month and Cal+1 volatility; Poland signals political cap-raising risk.

watchlist_dated.name: COP31 (Turkey host)
watchlist_dated.why: Cyprus row may delay carbon policy consensus; signals EUA curve sentiment shift.
